task_completion.description: Read the task mandate file to understand the requirements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of '~/Desktop/ro_plant_design_mandate.txt'.

task_completion.description: Open NASA Panoply and load the correct Sea Surface Temperature dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply GUI is open, and the dataset 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' is visible in the datasets/sources panel with the 'sst' variable selected.

task_completion.description: Generate a map plot for February Sea Surface Temperatures focused on Egypt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a map of Sea Surface Temperature. The time/array index should indicate February (e.g., Month 2 or index 1 depending on 0-indexing). The map view should be centered or zoomed in on Egypt (showing the Mediterranean and Red Sea).

task_completion.description: Generate a map plot for August Sea Surface Temperatures focused on Egypt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window showing a map of Sea Surface Temperature. The time/array index should indicate August (e.g., Month 8 or index 7). The map view should be centered or zoomed in on Egypt.

task_completion.description: Save the required map exports for February and August to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window showing the files 'egypt_coasts_feb.png' and 'egypt_coasts_aug.png' successfully created inside the '/home/ga/Documents/Desalination' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a text report detailing the seasonal temperature extremes and identifying the highest thermal stress environment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'thermal_envelope_report.txt' in '/home/ga/Documents/Desalination'. The text must include temperature values for the Mediterranean (~17.0 C Feb, ~27.1 C Aug) and Red Sea (~22.5 C Feb, ~29.2 C Aug), and explicitly identify the Red Sea as the highest peak thermal stress environment.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the visualizations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the Panoply Java application interface (menus, plot windows, arrays) rather than using a Python script or terminal commands to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Verify the exported map images are genuine Panoply outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the exported PNG files are previewed, they should exhibit standard Panoply visual characteristics (e.g., Panoply's default color bars, map borders, and title text formatting) rather than being generic downloaded images.

integrity.description: Verify the agent wrote the report based on observed data rather than copying a pre-existing solution file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen typing or constructing the 'thermal_envelope_report.txt' file in a text editor, extracting values from the Panoply plots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using NASA Panoply to analyze the 'sst' variable from '/home/ga/PanoplyData/sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc'. The expected outputs are '/home/ga/Documents/Desalination/egypt_coasts_feb.png', '/home/ga/Documents/Desalination/egypt_coasts_aug.png', and '/home/ga/Documents/Desalination/thermal_envelope_report.txt'. The correct temperature values to look for in the report are: Mediterranean coast ~17.0°C (Feb) and ~27.1°C (Aug); Red Sea ~22.5°C (Feb) and ~29.2°C (Aug). The report must classify the Red Sea as the highest peak thermal stress environment.
